title: Workflow Audit Checklist for Service Businesses
subtitle: Find the broken processes bleeding time and money from your $1M-$20M service business in 90 minutes

sections.title: Client Intake & Onboarding
sections.description: This is where most service businesses start losing time before they even deliver anything. Every manual step here multiplies across every new client.

items.text: Capture all new client information in one system instead of scattered across email, forms, and Slack
items.tip: Audit the last 5 clients: count how many systems their info lives in. More than 2 is a leak.

items.text: Template client onboarding tasks and assign them automatically on contract signature
items.tip: Trigger the template from your CRM stage change, not a human clicking a button.

items.text: Send welcome emails and kickoff materials automatically without someone hitting send
items.tip: A 24-hour delay on welcome sequences kills momentum. Aim for under 10 minutes post-signature.

items.text: Provision client portal, folder, and tool access without an IT ticket
items.tip: Use SCIM or API-based provisioning tied to your CRM. Manual access grants are a silent tax.

sections.title: Project & Job Management
sections.description: If your team is copy-pasting job details between systems or chasing down status updates, you are paying for coordination instead of execution.

items.text: Trigger tasks in all relevant systems automatically when a job or project is created
items.tip: Map every system a new job touches. Each manual copy-paste is 3-5 minutes of pure waste.

items.text: Route all status updates to one place instead of siloing them in email threads or DMs
items.tip: Pick one system as the source of truth and make it the only acceptable place for status.

items.text: Log every handoff between team members visibly instead of leaving it verbal or assumed
items.tip: Verbal handoffs lose 20-40% of context. Require a written transition note for every handoff.

items.text: Build project templates for repeatable work instead of recreating from scratch each time
items.tip: If you run the same type of project 3+ times per quarter, it needs a template.

sections.title: Communication & Notifications
sections.description: Most communication problems are actually workflow problems. If people need to ask for updates, the system is not doing its job.

items.text: Send clients automated updates at key milestones instead of waiting for someone to remember
items.tip: Define 4-6 milestones per engagement type and automate the notification at each one.

items.text: Trigger internal team notifications based on workflow events instead of manual pings
items.tip: If your team uses the phrase any update on, you have a notification workflow gap.

items.text: Maintain a single source of truth for client communication history across email, calls, and chat
items.tip: Sync external comms into your CRM automatically so nothing lives only in one person inbox.

items.text: Route escalations automatically when deadlines are missed or tasks stall beyond a threshold
items.tip: Set a stall threshold (e.g., 48 hours of no activity) and auto-notify the owner plus their manager.

sections.title: Billing & Invoicing
sections.description: Companies leave 10-15% of revenue on the table because invoicing is manual and inconsistent. This section finds that leak.

items.text: Generate invoices automatically from project milestones or time entries instead of manually each cycle
items.tip: Every manual invoice is a chance to undercount hours or miss a billable line item.

items.text: Send payment reminders automatically on a defined cadence instead of chasing AR by hand
items.tip: A 3-touch reminder sequence (day 1, 7, 14 past due) typically recovers 60%+ of late invoices.

items.text: Reconcile incoming payments to invoices automatically instead of matching them manually
items.tip: Connect your payment processor directly to your accounting system via native integration.

items.text: Capture scope changes and change orders in writing before any additional work begins
items.tip: Untracked scope creep is the single biggest margin killer in service businesses under $20M.

completion_cta.headline: You found the leaks. Now let's plug them.
completion_cta.description: If you flagged 4 or more broken items, you are likely losing 15-30 hours per week and thousands in revenue to manual work. Book a workflow audit call and I will map the 3 highest-ROI automations for your business, with estimated time and dollar savings for each.
